name: owned_endpoints_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_1_owned_endpoints_shared_across_containers(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    ep: EndpointPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c1),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(ep),
        container_perms[c2].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(ep),
    ensures
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(ep)
        && container_perms[c2].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(ep),
{
}
reason: No spec enforces disjointness of owned_endpoints across containers — the same endpoint can be owned by two containers simultaneously, violating the comment's "ONLY be referenced by threads in the subtree" intent

363 |     &&& new_container_perms[new_container_ptr].value().parent_rev_ptr.is_Some()
    |                                                                       ^^^^^^^

name: new_container_owned_procs_unconstrained
code:
proof fn phi_2_new_container_owned_procs_unconstrained(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    new_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    new_container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    p: ProcPtr,
)
    requires
        new_container_ensures(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr, new_container_ptr),
        new_container_perms[new_container_ptr].value().owned_procs@.contains(p),
    ensures
        new_container_perms[new_container_ptr].value().owned_procs@.contains(p),
{
}
reason: new_container_ensures never constrains owned_procs of the new container to be empty — a freshly created container can start with arbitrary processes

proof fn phi_3_new_container_scheduler_unconstrained(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,

    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    new_container_ptr: ContainerPtr,

)

    ensures

name: new_container_root_process_unconstrained
code:
proof fn phi_4_new_container_root_process_unconstrained(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    old_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    new_container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    new_container_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    p: ProcPtr,
)
    requires
        new_container_ensures(root_container, old_container_perms, new_container_perms, container_ptr, new_container_ptr),
        new_container_perms[new_container_ptr].value().root_process == Some(p),
    ensures
        new_container_perms[new_container_ptr].value().root_process == Some(p),
{
}
reason: new_container_ensures never constrains root_process of the new container to be None — a freshly created container can have an arbitrary root process

name: owned_cpus_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_5_owned_cpus_shared_across_containers(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    cpu: usize,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c1),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_cpus.set@.contains(cpu),
        container_perms[c2].value().owned_cpus.set@.contains(cpu),
    ensures
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_cpus.set@.contains(cpu)
        && container_perms[c2].value().owned_cpus.set@.contains(cpu),
{
}
reason: No spec enforces disjointness of owned_cpus across containers — the same CPU can be owned by two containers simultaneously, breaking exclusive CPU resource allocation
